title: Whole-transcriptome analysis of differentially expressed genes in mutant and normal capitula of Chrysanthemum morifolium
author: Hua Liu1, Chang Luo1, Dongliang Chen1, Yaqin Wang2, Shuang Guo1, Xiaoxi Chen1, Jingyi Bai1, Mingyuan Li1, Xinlei Huang, Xi Cheng1, Conglin Huang1,*
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Supplementary Figure S1 Flowers of chrysanthemum A The capitulum. B The disc floret. C The ray floret. 

 Supplementary Table S2 The DEGs specifically expressed in the mutant captula 

 Supplementary Table S3 The DEGs specifically expressed in the normal captula 

 Supplementary Table S4 The DEGs enriched in the “reproduction” term (GO0000003) 

 Supplementary Table S5 DEGs enriched in biological process 

 Supplementary Table S6 DEGs enriched in molecular function 

 Supplementary Table S7 DEGs enriched in cellular component 

 Supplementary Table S8 KEGG enrichment of DEGs down-regulated in the mutant capitula 

 Supplementary Table S9 KEGG enrichment of DEGs up-regulated in the mutant capitula 

 Supplementary Table S10 The important transcription factors genes that dramatically differentially expressed between normal and mutant capitula 

 Supplementary Table S11 The important genes involved in the photoperiod pathway in chrysanthemum 

 Supplementary Table S12 The important function genes in anthocyanin biosynthetic pathway in chrysanthemum 

 Supplementary Table S13 Primers used in real-time quantitative PCR of Chrysanthemum morifolium
